6132258,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxMzIyNTg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6132258?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs","html_url":"https://github.com/jdavcs","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"open_issues":61,"closed_issues":235,"state":"open","created_at":"2024-05-14T14:03:11Z","updated_at":"2024-11-12T18:35:52Z","due_on":null,"closed_at":null},"comments":18,"created_at":"2023-11-03T17:59:30Z","updated_at":"2024-11-12T19:02:55Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/16975","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Hi,
As part of our work at Oak Ridge National Lab, we've been using Galaxy for quite a while (some of us have attended GCC as well). We've also been doing some internal development on some features that users have requested here. One the most common ones was the ability to see live console output as jobs are running. This issue has been brought up before, for example https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/2332. But given, our users wanted it at the present, I took a stab at an implementation. My main goal was to minimize impact to the way Galaxy works now, so that there aren't any compatibility issues. I'll try to provide details about each part that was touched.

# Overview
The overall idea here was to add logic to allow the job manager to read the tool_stdout and tool_stderr files that are saved in the job directory, and return them as part of a status. The reason I put it into the status is because the UI already calls the status regularly form the JobInformation page, so I wouldn't have to make a new thread or anything. It also just kinda made sense to me that you might want it as part ofhte status of the job. To facilitate this, the api endpoint for getting job status was adjusted to allow parameters to select which part of the stdout/stderr (both work the same, so I'll just refer to stdout from here) that you want. There's `stdout_pos` which is the starting index in the stdout_file, and `stdout_length` which is how much of the stdout that you want (in chars). Because stdout could potentially be a relatively larger file, I didn't want to force people to read the whole file every time status is called. 

I then adjusted the UI for the job information view in a few different ways. First, I made the code blocks scrollable and set a max height for them. Then I moved the expand on click functionality over to only be on the expand icons, rather than the whole table row (if users would try and highlight a part of the stdout or click on the scroll bar to scroll, it would collapse the view). Lastly, I added an autoscroll feature that automatically scrolls the code blocks when the user is at the bottom of the stdout. If the user scrolls up, this is disabled. If they scroll back to the bottom, it starts again. 

The last thing I want to note is that as far as compatibility with job runners go, we almost exclusively use Pulsar for running jobs. *As is, this pr will only work for job runners that save their stdout to the job directory inside of the Galaxy.* Internally, we've added functionality for Pulsar to do this (the purpose of lib/galaxy/webapps/galaxy/api/job_files.py changes). I *did not* include those changes here, because that would require an additional pr to the Pulsar repository. Let me know if there's interest in seeing that however. It would also be nice to get some feedback on testing this.

I understand this is a pretty big change, and I imagine there are a lot of areas for improvement. Please let me know if this is something people are interested in helping with, or if this is a terrible way to try to do this, or whatever. It's worked for us so far internally, but I would love to have some feedback from here.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The best way to test is to start a tool that will run for some time with stdout.
  2. Start the tool and go to the JobInformation page for the job.
  3. Expand the Tool stdout by clicking on the expand icon to the right. 

## License
- [x] I agree to license these and all my past contributions to the core galaxy codebase under the [MIT license](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

To facilitate this, the api endpoint for getting job status was adjusted to allow parameters to select which part of the stdout/stderr (both work the same, so I'll just refer to stdout from here) that you want. There's `stdout_pos` which is the starting index in the stdout_file, and `stdout_length` which is how much of the stdout that you want (in chars). Because stdout could potentially be a relatively larger file, I didn't want to force people to read the whole file every time status is called. 

I then adjusted the UI for the job information view in a few different ways. First, I made the code blocks scrollable and set a max height for them. Then I moved the expand on click functionality over to only be on the expand icons, rather than the whole table row (if users would try and highlight a part of the stdout or click on the scroll bar to scroll, it would collapse the view). Lastly, I added an autoscroll feature that automatically scrolls the code blocks when the user is at the bottom of the stdout. If the user scrolls up, this is disabled. If they scroll back to the bottom, it starts again. 

The last thing I want to note is that as far as compatibility with job runners go, we almost exclusively use Pulsar for running jobs. *As is, this pr will only work for job runners that save their stdout to the job directory inside of the Galaxy.* Internally, we've added functionality for Pulsar to do this (the purpose of lib/galaxy/webapps/galaxy/api/job_files.py changes). I *did not* include those changes here, because that would require an additional pr to the Pulsar repository. Let me know if there's interest in seeing that however. It would also be nice to get some feedback on testing this.

I understand this is a pretty big change, and I imagine there are a lot of areas for improvement. Please let me know if this is something people are interested in helping with, or if this is a terrible way to try to do this, or whatever. It's worked for us so far internally, but I would love to have some feedback from here.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The best way to test is to start a tool that will run for some time with stdout.
  2. Start the tool and go to the JobInformation page for the job.
  3. Expand the Tool stdout by clicking on the expand icon to the right.

As part of our work at Oak Ridge National Lab, we've been using Galaxy for quite a while (some of us have attended GCC as well). We've also been doing some internal development on some features that users have requested here. One the most common ones was the ability to see live console output as jobs are running. This issue has been brought up before, for example https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/2332. But given, our users wanted it at the present, I took a stab at an implementation. My main goal was to minimize impact to the way Galaxy works now, so that there aren't any compatibility issues. I'll try to provide details about each part that was touched.

# Overview
The overall idea here was to add logic to allow the job manager to read the tool_stdout and tool_stderr files that are saved in the job directory, and return them as part of a status. The reason I put it into the status is because the UI already calls the status regularly form the JobInformation page, so I wouldn't have to make a new thread or anything. It also just kinda made sense to me that you might want it as part ofhte status of the job. To facilitate this, the api endpoint for getting job status was adjusted to allow parameters to select which part of the stdout/stderr (both work the same, so I'll just refer to stdout from here) that you want. There's `stdout_pos` which is the starting index in the stdout_file, and `stdout_length` which is how much of the stdout that you want (in chars). Because stdout could potentially be a relatively larger file, I didn't want to force people to read the whole file every time status is called. 

I then adjusted the UI for the job information view in a few different ways. First, I made the code blocks scrollable and set a max height for them. Then I moved the expand on click functionality over to only be on the expand icons, rather than the whole table row (if users would try and highlight a part of the stdout or click on the scroll bar to scroll, it would collapse the view). Lastly, I added an autoscroll feature that automatically scrolls the code blocks when the user is at the bottom of the stdout. If the user scrolls up, this is disabled. If they scroll back to the bottom, it starts again. 

The last thing I want to note is that as far as compatibility with job runners go, we almost exclusively use Pulsar for running jobs. *As is, this pr will only work for job runners that save their stdout to the job directory inside of the Galaxy.* Internally, we've added functionality for Pulsar to do this (the purpose of lib/galaxy/webapps/galaxy/api/job_files.py changes). I *did not* include those changes here, because that would require an additional pr to the Pulsar repository. Let me know if there's interest in seeing that however. It would also be nice to get some feedback on testing this.

I understand this is a pretty big change, and I imagine there are a lot of areas for improvement. Please let me know if this is something people are interested in helping with, or if this is a terrible way to try to do this, or whatever. It's worked for us so far internally, but I would love to have some feedback from here.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The best way to test is to start a tool that will run for some time with stdout.
  2. Start the tool and go to the JobInformation page for the job.
  3. Expand the Tool stdout by clicking on the expand icon to the right.

lcjYxMzIyNTg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6132258?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs","html_url":"https://github.com/jdavcs","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"open_issues":31,"closed_issues":6,"state":"open","created_at":"2024-05-14T14:03:11Z","updated_at":"2024-06-13T16:40:37Z","due_on":null,"closed_at":null},"comments":11,"created_at":"2023-11-03T17:59:30Z","updated_at":"2024-06-14T15:28:34Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/16975","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Hi,
As part of our work at Oak Ridge National Lab, we've been using Galaxy for quite a while (some of us have attended GCC as well). We've also been doing some internal development on some features that users have requested here. One the most common ones was the ability to see live console output as jobs are running. This issue has been brought up before, for example https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/2332. But given, our users wanted it at the present, I took a stab at an implementation. My main goal was to minimize impact to the way Galaxy works now, so that there aren't any compatibility issues. I'll try to provide details about each part that was touched.

# Overview
The overall idea here was to add logic to allow the job manager to read the tool_stdout and tool_stderr files that are saved in the job directory, and return them as part of a status. The reason I put it into the status is because the UI already calls the status regularly form the JobInformation page, so I wouldn't have to make a new thread or anything. It also just kinda made sense to me that you might want it as part ofhte status of the job. To facilitate this, the api endpoint for getting job status was adjusted to allow parameters to select which part of the stdout/stderr (both work the same, so I'll just refer to stdout from here) that you want. There's `stdout_pos` which is the starting index in the stdout_file, and `stdout_length` which is how much of the stdout that you want (in chars). Because stdout could potentially be a relatively larger file, I didn't want to force people to read the whole file every time status is called. 

I then adjusted the UI for the job information view in a few different ways. First, I made the code blocks scrollable and set a max height for them. Then I moved the expand on click functionality over to only be on the expand icons, rather than the whole table row (if users would try and highlight a part of the stdout or click on the scroll bar to scroll, it would collapse the view). Lastly, I added an autoscroll feature that automatically scrolls the code blocks when the user is at the bottom of the stdout. If the user scrolls up, this is disabled. If they scroll back to the bottom, it starts again. 

The last thing I want to note is that as far as compatibility with job runners go, we almost exclusively use Pulsar for running jobs. *As is, this pr will only work for job runners that save their stdout to the job directory inside of the Galaxy.* Internally, we've added functionality for Pulsar to do this (the purpose of lib/galaxy/webapps/galaxy/api/job_files.py changes). I *did not* include those changes here, because that would require an additional pr to the Pulsar repository. Let me know if there's interest in seeing that however. It would also be nice to get some feedback on testing this.

I understand this is a pretty big change, and I imagine there are a lot of areas for improvement. Please let me know if this is something people are interested in helping with, or if this is a terrible way to try to do this, or whatever. It's worked for us so far internally, but I would love to have some feedback from here.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The best way to test is to start a tool that will run for some time with stdout.
  2. Start the tool and go to the JobInformation page for the job.
  3. Expand the Tool stdout by clicking on the expand icon to the right. 

## License
- [x] I agree to license these and all my past contributions to the core galaxy codebase under the [MIT license](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The best way to test is to start a tool that will run for some time with stdout.
  2. Start the tool and go to the JobInformation page for the job.
  3. Expand the Tool stdout by clicking on the expand icon to the right.

"title":"Feature reporting","user":{"login":"gecage952","id":30890419,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwODkwNDE5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30890419?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952","html_url":"https://github.com/gecage952","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Hi,
As part of our work at Oak Ridge National Lab, we've been using Galaxy for quite a while (some of us have attended GCC as well). We've also been doing some internal development on some features that users have requested here. One the most common ones was the ability to see live console output as jobs are running. This issue has been brought up before, for example https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/2332. But given, our users wanted it at the present, I took a stab at an implementation. My main goal was to minimize impact to the way Galaxy works now, so that there aren't any compatibility issues. I'll try to provide details about each part that was touched.

# Overview
The overall idea here was to add logic to allow the job manager to read the tool_stdout and tool_stderr files that are saved in the job directory, and return them as part of a status. The reason I put it into the status is because the UI already calls the status regularly form the JobInformation page, so I wouldn't have to make a new thread or anything. It also just kinda made sense to me that you might want it as part ofhte status of the job. To facilitate this, the api endpoint for getting job status was adjusted to allow parameters to select which part of the stdout/stderr (both work the same, so I'll just refer to stdout from here) that you want. There's `stdout_pos` which is the starting index in the stdout_file, and `stdout_length` which is how much of the stdout that you want (in chars). Because stdout could potentially be a relatively larger file, I didn't want to force people to read the whole file every time status is called. 

I then adjusted the UI for the job information view in a few different ways. First, I made the code blocks scrollable and set a max height for them. Then I moved the expand on click functionality over to only be on the expand icons, rather than the whole table row (if users would try and highlight a part of the stdout or click on the scroll bar to scroll, it would collapse the view). Lastly, I added an autoscroll feature that automatically scrolls the code blocks when the user is at the bottom of the stdout. If the user scrolls up, this is disabled. If they scroll back to the bottom, it starts again. 

The last thing I want to note is that as far as compatibility with job runners go, we almost exclusively use Pulsar for running jobs. *As is, this pr will only work for job runners that save their stdout to the job directory inside of the Galaxy.* Internally, we've added functionality for Pulsar to do this (the purpose of lib/galaxy/webapps/galaxy/api/job_files.py changes). I *did not* include those changes here, because that would require an additional pr to the Pulsar repository. Let me know if there's interest in seeing that however. It would also be nice to get some feedback on testing this.

I understand this is a pretty big change, and I imagine there are a lot of areas for improvement. Please let me know if this is something people are interested in helping with, or if this is a terrible way to try to do this, or whatever. It's worked for us so far internally, but I would love to have some feedback from here.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The best way to test is to start a tool that will run for some time with stdout.
  2. Start the tool and go to the JobInformation page for the job.
  3. Expand the Tool stdout by clicking on the expand icon to the right.

stdout live reporting","user":{"login":"gecage952","id":30890419,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwODkwNDE5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30890419?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952","html_url":"https://github.com/gecage952","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Hi,
As part of our work at Oak Ridge National Lab, we've been using Galaxy for quite a while (some of us have attended GCC as well). We've also been doing some internal development on some features that users have requested here. One the most common ones was the ability to see live console output as jobs are running. This issue has been brought up before, for example https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/2332. But given, our users wanted it at the present, I took a stab at an implementation. My main goal was to minimize impact to the way Galaxy works now, so that there aren't any compatibility issues. I'll try to provide details about each part that was touched.

# Overview
The overall idea here was to add logic to allow the job manager to read the tool_stdout and tool_stderr files that are saved in the job directory, and return them as part of a status. The reason I put it into the status is because the UI already calls the status regularly form the JobInformation page, so I wouldn't have to make a new thread or anything. It also just kinda made sense to me that you might want it as part ofhte status of the job. To facilitate this, the api endpoint for getting job status was adjusted to allow parameters to select which part of the stdout/stderr (both work the same, so I'll just refer to stdout from here) that you want. There's `stdout_pos` which is the starting index in the stdout_file, and `stdout_length` which is how much of the stdout that you want (in chars). Because stdout could potentially be a relatively larger file, I didn't want to force people to read the whole file every time status is called. 

I then adjusted the UI for the job information view in a few different ways. First, I made the code blocks scrollable and set a max height for them. Then I moved the expand on click functionality over to only be on the expand icons, rather than the whole table row (if users would try and highlight a part of the stdout or click on the scroll bar to scroll, it would collapse the view). Lastly, I added an autoscroll feature that automatically scrolls the code blocks when the user is at the bottom of the stdout. If the user scrolls up, this is disabled. If they scroll back to the bottom, it starts again. 

The last thing I want to note is that as far as compatibility with job runners go, we almost exclusively use Pulsar for running jobs. *As is, this pr will only work for job runners that save their stdout to the job directory inside of the Galaxy.* Internally, we've added functionality for Pulsar to do this (the purpose of lib/galaxy/webapps/galaxy/api/job_files.py changes). I *did not* include those changes here, because that would require an additional pr to the Pulsar repository. Let me know if there's interest in seeing that however. It would also be nice to get some feedback on testing this.

I understand this is a pretty big change, and I imagine there are a lot of areas for improvement. Please let me know if this is something people are interested in helping with, or if this is a terrible way to try to do this, or whatever. It's worked for us so far internally, but I would love to have some feedback from here.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The best way to test is to start a tool that will run for some time with stdout.
  2. Start the tool and go to the JobInformation page for the job.
  3. Expand the Tool stdout by clicking on the expand icon to the right.

reporting","user":{"login":"gecage952","id":30890419,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwODkwNDE5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30890419?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952","html_url":"https://github.com/gecage952","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Hi,
As part of our work at Oak Ridge National Lab, we've been using Galaxy for quite a while (some of us have attended GCC as well). We've also been doing some internal development on some features that users have requested here. One the most common ones was the ability to see live console output as jobs are running. This issue has been brought up before, for example https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/2332. But given, our users wanted it at the present, I took a stab at an implementation. My main goal was to minimize impact to the way Galaxy works now, so that there aren't any compatibility issues. I'll try to provide details about each part that was touched.

# Overview
The overall idea here was to add logic to allow the job manager to read the tool_stdout and tool_stderr files that are saved in the job directory, and return them as part of a status. The reason I put it into the status is because the UI already calls the status regularly form the JobInformation page, so I wouldn't have to make a new thread or anything. It also just kinda made sense to me that you might want it as part ofhte status of the job. To facilitate this, the api endpoint for getting job status was adjusted to allow parameters to select which part of the stdout/stderr (both work the same, so I'll just refer to stdout from here) that you want. There's `stdout_pos` which is the starting index in the stdout_file, and `stdout_length` which is how much of the stdout that you want (in chars). Because stdout could potentially be a relatively larger file, I didn't want to force people to read the whole file every time status is called. 

I then adjusted the UI for the job information view in a few different ways. First, I made the code blocks scrollable and set a max height for them. Then I moved the expand on click functionality over to only be on the expand icons, rather than the whole table row (if users would try and highlight a part of the stdout or click on the scroll bar to scroll, it would collapse the view). Lastly, I added an autoscroll feature that automatically scrolls the code blocks when the user is at the bottom of the stdout. If the user scrolls up, this is disabled. If they scroll back to the bottom, it starts again. 

The last thing I want to note is that as far as compatibility with job runners go, we almost exclusively use Pulsar for running jobs. *As is, this pr will only work for job runners that save their stdout to the job directory inside of the Galaxy.* Internally, we've added functionality for Pulsar to do this (the purpose of lib/galaxy/webapps/galaxy/api/job_files.py changes). I *did not* include those changes here, because that would require an additional pr to the Pulsar repository. Let me know if there's interest in seeing that however. It would also be nice to get some feedback on testing this.

I understand this is a pretty big change, and I imagine there are a lot of areas for improvement. Please let me know if this is something people are interested in helping with, or if this is a terrible way to try to do this, or whatever. It's worked for us so far internally, but I would love to have some feedback from here.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The best way to test is to start a tool that will run for some time with stdout.
  2. Start the tool and go to the JobInformation page for the job.
  3. Expand the Tool stdout by clicking on the expand icon to the right.

lcjYxMzIyNTg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6132258?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs","html_url":"https://github.com/jdavcs","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"open_issues":31,"closed_issues":2,"state":"open","created_at":"2024-05-14T14:03:11Z","updated_at":"2024-05-23T12:51:03Z","due_on":null,"closed_at":null},"comments":10,"created_at":"2023-11-03T17:59:30Z","updated_at":"2024-05-29T19:07:18Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/16975","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Hi,
As part of our work at Oak Ridge National Lab, we've been using Galaxy for quite a while (some of us have attended GCC as well). We've also been doing some internal development on some features that users have requested here. One the most common ones was the ability to see live console output as jobs are running. This issue has been brought up before, for example https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/2332. But given, our users wanted it at the present, I took a stab at an implementation. My main goal was to minimize impact to the way Galaxy works now, so that there aren't any compatibility issues. I'll try to provide details about each part that was touched.

# Overview
The overall idea here was to add logic to allow the job manager to read the tool_stdout and tool_stderr files that are saved in the job directory, and return them as part of a status. The reason I put it into the status is because the UI already calls the status regularly form the JobInformation page, so I wouldn't have to make a new thread or anything. It also just kinda made sense to me that you might want it as part ofhte status of the job. To facilitate this, the api endpoint for getting job status was adjusted to allow parameters to select which part of the stdout/stderr (both work the same, so I'll just refer to stdout from here) that you want. There's `stdout_pos` which is the starting index in the stdout_file, and `stdout_length` which is how much of the stdout that you want (in chars). Because stdout could potentially be a relatively larger file, I didn't want to force people to read the whole file every time status is called. 

I then adjusted the UI for the job information view in a few different ways. First, I made the code blocks scrollable and set a max height for them. Then I moved the expand on click functionality over to only be on the expand icons, rather than the whole table row (if users would try and highlight a part of the stdout or click on the scroll bar to scroll, it would collapse the view). Lastly, I added an autoscroll feature that automatically scrolls the code blocks when the user is at the bottom of the stdout. If the user scrolls up, this is disabled. If they scroll back to the bottom, it starts again. 

The last thing I want to note is that as far as compatibility with job runners go, we almost exclusively use Pulsar for running jobs. *As is, this pr will only work for job runners that save their stdout to the job directory inside of the Galaxy.* Internally, we've added functionality for Pulsar to do this (the purpose of lib/galaxy/webapps/galaxy/api/job_files.py changes). I *did not* include those changes here, because that would require an additional pr to the Pulsar repository. Let me know if there's interest in seeing that however. It would also be nice to get some feedback on testing this.

I understand this is a pretty big change, and I imagine there are a lot of areas for improvement. Please let me know if this is something people are interested in helping with, or if this is a terrible way to try to do this, or whatever. It's worked for us so far internally, but I would love to have some feedback from here.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The best way to test is to start a tool that will run for some time with stdout.
  2. Start the tool and go to the JobInformation page for the job.
  3. Expand the Tool stdout by clicking on the expand icon to the right.

reporting","user":{"login":"gecage952","id":30890419,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwODkwNDE5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30890419?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952","html_url":"https://github.com/gecage952","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":276556910,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNzY1NTY5MTA=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/kind/feature","name":"kind/feature","color":"84b6eb","default":false,"description":null},{"id":276558486,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNzY1NTg0ODY=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/area/API","name":"area/API","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":null},{"id":276558748,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNzY1NTg3NDg=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/area/jobs","name":"area/jobs","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":8,"created_at":"2023-11-03T17:59:30Z","updated_at":"2024-03-22T16:09:35Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":true,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/16975","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Hi,
As part of our work at Oak Ridge National Lab, we've been using Galaxy for quite a while (some of us have attended GCC as well). We've also been doing some internal development on some features that users have requested here. One the most common ones was the ability to see live console output as jobs are running. This issue has been brought up before, for example https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/2332. But given, our users wanted it at the present, I took a stab at an implementation. My main goal was to minimize impact to the way Galaxy works now, so that there aren't any compatibility issues. I'll try to provide details about each part that was touched.

# Overview
The overall idea here was to add logic to allow the job manager to read the tool_stdout and tool_stderr files that are saved in the job directory, and return them as part of a status. The reason I put it into the status is because the UI already calls the status regularly form the JobInformation page, so I wouldn't have to make a new thread or anything. It also just kinda made sense to me that you might want it as part ofhte status of the job. To facilitate this, the api endpoint for getting job status was adjusted to allow parameters to select which part of the stdout/stderr (both work the same, so I'll just refer to stdout from here) that you want. There's `stdout_pos` which is the starting index in the stdout_file, and `stdout_length` which is how much of the stdout that you want (in chars). Because stdout could potentially be a relatively larger file, I didn't want to force people to read the whole file every time status is called. 

I then adjusted the UI for the job information view in a few different ways. First, I made the code blocks scrollable and set a max height for them. Then I moved the expand on click functionality over to only be on the expand icons, rather than the whole table row (if users would try and highlight a part of the stdout or click on the scroll bar to scroll, it would collapse the view). Lastly, I added an autoscroll feature that automatically scrolls the code blocks when the user is at the bottom of the stdout. If the user scrolls up, this is disabled. If they scroll back to the bottom, it starts again. 

The last thing I want to note is that as far as compatibility with job runners go, we almost exclusively use Pulsar for running jobs. *As is, this pr will only work for job runners that save their stdout to the job directory inside of the Galaxy.* Internally, we've added functionality for Pulsar to do this (the purpose of lib/galaxy/webapps/galaxy/api/job_files.py changes). I *did not* include those changes here, because that would require an additional pr to the Pulsar repository. Let me know if there's interest in seeing that however. It would also be nice to get some feedback on testing this.

I understand this is a pretty big change, and I imagine there are a lot of areas for improvement. Please let me know if this is something people are interested in helping with, or if this is a terrible way to try to do this, or whatever. It's worked for us so far internally, but I would love to have some feedback from here.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The best way to test is to start a tool that will run for some time with stdout.
  2. Start the tool and go to the JobInformation page for the job.
  3. Expand the Tool stdout by clicking on the expand icon to the right. 

## License
- [x] I agree to license these and all my past contributions to the core galaxy codebase under the [MIT license](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

{"action":"opened","number":16975,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/16975","id":1586493675,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAdw-Nc5ej_jr","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16975","number":16975,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[WIP] Feature - stdout live reporting","user":{"login":"gecage952","id":30890419,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwODkwNDE5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30890419?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952","html_url":"https://github.com/gecage952","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Hi,
As part of our work at Oak Ridge National Lab, we've been using Galaxy for quite a while (some of us have attended GCC as well). We've also been doing some internal development on some features that users have requested here. One the most common ones was the ability to see live console output as jobs are running. This issue has been brought up before, for example https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/2332. But given, our users wanted it at the present, I took a stab at an implementation. My main goal was to minimize impact to the way Galaxy works now, so that there aren't any compatibility issues. I'll try to provide details about each part that was touched.

# Overview
The overall idea here was to add logic to allow the job manager to read the tool_stdout and tool_stderr files that are saved in the job directory, and return them as part of a status. The reason I put it into the status is because the UI already calls the status regularly form the JobInformation page, so I wouldn't have to make a new thread or anything. It also just kinda made sense to me that you might want it as part ofhte status of the job. To facilitate this, the api endpoint for getting job status was adjusted to allow parameters to select which part of the stdout/stderr (both work the same, so I'll just refer to stdout from here) that you want. There's `stdout_pos` which is the starting index in the stdout_file, and `stdout_length` which is how much of the stdout that you want (in chars). Because stdout could potentially be a relatively larger file, I didn't want to force people to read the whole file every time status is called. 

I then adjusted the UI for the job information view in a few different ways. First, I made the code blocks scrollable and set a max height for them. Then I moved the expand on click functionality over to only be on the expand icons, rather than the whole table row (if users would try and highlight a part of the stdout or click on the scroll bar to scroll, it would collapse the view). Lastly, I added an autoscroll feature that automatically scrolls the code blocks when the user is at the bottom of the stdout. If the user scrolls up, this is disabled. If they scroll back to the bottom, it starts again. 

The last thing I want to note is that as far as compatibility with job runners go, we almost exclusively use Pulsar for running jobs. *As is, this pr will only work for job runners that save their stdout to the job directory inside of the Galaxy.* Internally, we've added functionality for Pulsar to do this (the purpose of lib/galaxy/webapps/galaxy/api/job_files.py changes). I *did not* include those changes here, because that would require an additional pr to the Pulsar repository. Let me know if there's interest in seeing that however. It would also be nice to get some feedback and testing this.

I understand this is a pretty big change, and I imagine there are a lot of areas for improvement. Please let me know if this is something people are interested in helping with, or if this is a terrible way to try to do this, or whatever. It's worked for us so far internally, but I would love to have some feedback from here.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The best way to test is to start a tool that will run for some time with stdout.
  2. Start the tool and go to the JobInformation page for the job.
  3. Expand the Tool stdout by clicking on the expand icon to the right.

- [x] I agree to license these and all my past contributions to the core galaxy codebase under the [MIT license](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

reporting","user":{"login":"gecage952","id":30890419,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwODkwNDE5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30890419?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952","html_url":"https://github.com/gecage952","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":276556910,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNzY1NTY5MTA=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/kind/feature","name":"kind/feature","color":"84b6eb","default":false,"description":null},{"id":276558486,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNzY1NTg0ODY=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/area/API","name":"area/API","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":null},{"id":276558748,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNzY1NTg3NDg=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/area/jobs","name":"area/jobs","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2023-11-03T17:59:30Z","updated_at":"2023-11-07T17:30:32Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/16975","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Hi,
As part of our work at Oak Ridge National Lab, we've been using Galaxy for quite a while (some of us have attended GCC as well). We've also been doing some internal development on some features that users have requested here. One the most common ones was the ability to see live console output as jobs are running. This issue has been brought up before, for example https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/2332. But given, our users wanted it at the present, I took a stab at an implementation. My main goal was to minimize impact to the way Galaxy works now, so that there aren't any compatibility issues. I'll try to provide details about each part that was touched.

# Overview
The overall idea here was to add logic to allow the job manager to read the tool_stdout and tool_stderr files that are saved in the job directory, and return them as part of a status. The reason I put it into the status is because the UI already calls the status regularly form the JobInformation page, so I wouldn't have to make a new thread or anything. It also just kinda made sense to me that you might want it as part ofhte status of the job. To facilitate this, the api endpoint for getting job status was adjusted to allow parameters to select which part of the stdout/stderr (both work the same, so I'll just refer to stdout from here) that you want. There's `stdout_pos` which is the starting index in the stdout_file, and `stdout_length` which is how much of the stdout that you want (in chars). Because stdout could potentially be a relatively larger file, I didn't want to force people to read the whole file every time status is called. 

I then adjusted the UI for the job information view in a few different ways. First, I made the code blocks scrollable and set a max height for them. Then I moved the expand on click functionality over to only be on the expand icons, rather than the whole table row (if users would try and highlight a part of the stdout or click on the scroll bar to scroll, it would collapse the view). Lastly, I added an autoscroll feature that automatically scrolls the code blocks when the user is at the bottom of the stdout. If the user scrolls up, this is disabled. If they scroll back to the bottom, it starts again. 

The last thing I want to note is that as far as compatibility with job runners go, we almost exclusively use Pulsar for running jobs. *As is, this pr will only work for job runners that save their stdout to the job directory inside of the Galaxy.* Internally, we've added functionality for Pulsar to do this (the purpose of lib/galaxy/webapps/galaxy/api/job_files.py changes). I *did not* include those changes here, because that would require an additional pr to the Pulsar repository. Let me know if there's interest in seeing that however. It would also be nice to get some feedback on testing this.

I understand this is a pretty big change, and I imagine there are a lot of areas for improvement. Please let me know if this is something people are interested in helping with, or if this is a terrible way to try to do this, or whatever. It's worked for us so far internally, but I would love to have some feedback from here.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The best way to test is to start a tool that will run for some time with stdout.
  2. Start the tool and go to the JobInformation page for the job.
  3. Expand the Tool stdout by clicking on the expand icon to the right. 

## License

As a Pulsar deployer, I'm also interested in the Pulsar part and how this works in practice. Are you using the MQ deployment of Pulsar?

Yeah, rabbitmq. 
The general idea of the pulsar piece is to send the stdout/stderr files to the job_files api endpoint periodically. To make sure we aren't sending the entire file every time, we keep track of the last position in a map. Then when it's time to send the next chunk, we seek to that position in the files, post it to the job_files endpoint which appends it to the file in the Galaxy job directory. We also considered using the message queue for this instead of the api, but ended up not going that direction.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/comments/1799296817/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","number":345,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/pulsar/pulls/345","id":1607045531,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAUCz8c5fyZGb","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/pulsar/pull/345","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/pulsar/pull/345.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/pulsar/pull/345.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/pulsar/issues/345","number":345,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[WIP] Feature: Send stdout and stderr to Galaxy while job is running","user":{"login":"gecage952","id":30890419,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwODkwNDE5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30890419?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952","html_url":"https://github.com/gecage952","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Hey,
So this is related to this pr in the main Galaxy repo: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975.
The changes facilitate sending both stdout and stderr to Galaxy while a job is running for the purposes of displaying said stdout inside of Galaxy while the job is running. 

The main changes include adding two parameters to the `app.yml` config: `send_stdout_update` which is a boolean, and `stdout_update_interval` which is a float. The first controls whether Pulsar will send stdout/stderr or not, the second is the interval (in seconds) between updates. 

The way that the files is sent is through the files endpoint in Galaxy. In order to not send the entire file each time, in a dict I keep track of the position in the stdout/stderr file that the last update read up to. I then only send the new part of the stdout file. 

After the job is finished, I send any stdout/stderr left that has not been sent. In the final status message send over the broker, instead of including stdout there, I set the stdout and stderr fields there to None, so that it doesn't send the whole file again. In Galaxy, there are a couple of changes in the Pulsar job runner that check if those fields are None, and if so load the stdout from the job directory there.

Like with the other pr, this was done mostly with the intent of not messing around with existing functionality, which is why we didn't want to use messages to send it. Also, like the other pr, any feedback is welcome.

reporting","user":{"login":"gecage952","id":30890419,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwODkwNDE5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30890419?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952","html_url":"https://github.com/gecage952","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":276556910,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNzY1NTY5MTA=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/kind/feature","name":"kind/feature","color":"84b6eb","default":false,"description":null},{"id":276558486,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNzY1NTg0ODY=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/area/API","name":"area/API","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":null},{"id":276558748,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNzY1NTg3NDg=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/area/jobs","name":"area/jobs","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":5,"created_at":"2023-11-03T17:59:30Z","updated_at":"2023-11-17T19:52:33Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/16975","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/16975.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Hi,
As part of our work at Oak Ridge National Lab, we've been using Galaxy for quite a while (some of us have attended GCC as well). We've also been doing some internal development on some features that users have requested here. One the most common ones was the ability to see live console output as jobs are running. This issue has been brought up before, for example https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/2332. But given, our users wanted it at the present, I took a stab at an implementation. My main goal was to minimize impact to the way Galaxy works now, so that there aren't any compatibility issues. I'll try to provide details about each part that was touched.

# Overview
The overall idea here was to add logic to allow the job manager to read the tool_stdout and tool_stderr files that are saved in the job directory, and return them as part of a status. The reason I put it into the status is because the UI already calls the status regularly form the JobInformation page, so I wouldn't have to make a new thread or anything. It also just kinda made sense to me that you might want it as part ofhte status of the job. To facilitate this, the api endpoint for getting job status was adjusted to allow parameters to select which part of the stdout/stderr (both work the same, so I'll just refer to stdout from here) that you want. There's `stdout_pos` which is the starting index in the stdout_file, and `stdout_length` which is how much of the stdout that you want (in chars). Because stdout could potentially be a relatively larger file, I didn't want to force people to read the whole file every time status is called. 

I then adjusted the UI for the job information view in a few different ways. First, I made the code blocks scrollable and set a max height for them. Then I moved the expand on click functionality over to only be on the expand icons, rather than the whole table row (if users would try and highlight a part of the stdout or click on the scroll bar to scroll, it would collapse the view). Lastly, I added an autoscroll feature that automatically scrolls the code blocks when the user is at the bottom of the stdout. If the user scrolls up, this is disabled. If they scroll back to the bottom, it starts again. 

The last thing I want to note is that as far as compatibility with job runners go, we almost exclusively use Pulsar for running jobs. *As is, this pr will only work for job runners that save their stdout to the job directory inside of the Galaxy.* Internally, we've added functionality for Pulsar to do this (the purpose of lib/galaxy/webapps/galaxy/api/job_files.py changes). I *did not* include those changes here, because that would require an additional pr to the Pulsar repository. Let me know if there's interest in seeing that however. It would also be nice to get some feedback on testing this.

I understand this is a pretty big change, and I imagine there are a lot of areas for improvement. Please let me know if this is something people are interested in helping with, or if this is a terrible way to try to do this, or whatever. It's worked for us so far internally, but I would love to have some feedback from here.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The best way to test is to start a tool that will run for some time with stdout.
  2. Start the tool and go to the JobInformation page for the job.
  3. Expand the Tool stdout by clicking on the expand icon to the right. 

## License

Issue: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13605

This pr is to add Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) support when using OIDC auth. Essentially, it's a few extra parameters added to some OAuth requests for extra security. Some OIDC providers recommend having support for these parameters, and it may be required for some. 

I tested this pr manually with Keycloak using a setup that required PKCE parameters, and then both a Keycloak setup and a Google setup  that did not require them making sure existing functionality was not affected. I also added a unit test with the other OIDC auth tests that followed the pattern of other already existing Galaxy unit tests in `test_custos_authnz.py`. 

I did add a file called `pkce_utils.py` in `galaxy/lib/galaxy/authnz`. This file is just taken from a python package (https://pypi.org/project/pkce/), and I included the license from that, because I wasn't sure how to handle the dependency management.. Let me know if this would need to be changed. I did not make changes to any of the docs. This is my first pr to this project, so let me know if I need to change anything.

The spec for PKCE is here: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7636
More info can be found here: https://www.oauth.com/oauth2-servers/pkce/


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [x] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [x] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. Make sure the OIDC support is enabled in `galaxy.yml`. Instructions are found here: https://galaxyproject.org/authnz/config/oidc/#enable-oidc-based-login 
  2. For testing with Keycloak, in `oidc_backends_config`, under the Keycloak configuration add a xml attribute called 
`pkce_support` like the following to an existing Keycloak config:
  
 ```oidc_backends_config.xml

    <provider name="Keycloak">
        <!-- The URL should include the base keycloak path as well as the realm -->
        <url>http://localhost-uri:8080/auth/realms/master/</url>
        <pkce_support>true</pkce_support>
         ...
 ```
  3. Configure Keycloak to use PKCE. Instructions can be found in the Keycloak docs by searching for PKCE: https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_oidc_clients. 
  4. Start Galaxy, and open up the home page.
  5. Click on the login link in the top right.
  6. Choose to sign in with KeyCloak. 
  7. Sign in as a Keycloak user.
  8. You should be redirected back to the Galaxy homepage and signed in.

support","user":{"login":"gecage952","id":30890419,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwODkwNDE5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30890419?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952","html_url":"https://github.com/gecage952","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Relevant Issue: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13605

This pr is to add Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) support when using OIDC auth. Essentially, it's a few extra parameters added to some OAuth requests for extra security. Some OIDC providers recommend having support for these parameters, and it may be required for some. 

I tested this pr manually with Keycloak using a setup that required PKCE parameters, and then both a Keycloak setup and a Google setup  that did not require them making sure existing functionality was not affected. I also added a unit test with the other OIDC auth tests that followed the pattern of other already existing Galaxy unit tests in `test_custos_authnz.py`. 

I did add a file called `pkce_utils.py` in `galaxy/lib/galaxy/authnz`. This file is just taken from a python package (https://pypi.org/project/pkce/), and I included the license from that, because I wasn't sure how to handle the dependency management.. Let me know if this would need to be changed. I did not make changes to any of the docs. This is my first pr to this project, so let me know if I need to change anything.

The spec for PKCE is here: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7636
More info can be found here: https://www.oauth.com/oauth2-servers/pkce/


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [x] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [x] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. Make sure the OIDC support is enabled in `galaxy.yml`. Instructions are found here: https://galaxyproject.org/authnz/config/oidc/#enable-oidc-based-login 
  2. For testing with Keycloak, in `oidc_backends_config`, under the Keycloak configuration add a xml attribute called 
`pkce_support` like the following to an existing Keycloak config:
  
 ```oidc_backends_config.xml

    <provider name="Keycloak">
        <!-- The URL should include the base keycloak path as well as the realm -->
        <url>http://localhost-uri:8080/auth/realms/master/</url>
        <pkce_support>true</pkce_support>
         ...
 ```
  3. Configure Keycloak to use PKCE. Instructions can be found in the Keycloak docs by searching for PKCE: https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_oidc_clients. 
  4. Start Galaxy, and open up the home page.
  5. Click on the login link in the top right.
  6. Choose to sign in with KeyCloak. 
  7. Sign in as a Keycloak user.
  8. You should be redirected back to the Galaxy homepage and signed in.

support","user":{"login":"gecage952","id":30890419,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwODkwNDE5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30890419?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952","html_url":"https://github.com/gecage952","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Relevant Issue: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13605

This pr is to add Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) support when using OIDC auth. Essentially, it's a few extra parameters added to some OAuth requests for extra security. Some OIDC providers recommend having support for these parameters, and it may be required for some. 

I tested this pr manually with Keycloak using a setup that required PKCE parameters, and then both a Keycloak setup and a Google setup  that did not require them making sure existing functionality was not affected. I also added a unit test with the other OIDC auth tests that followed the pattern of other already existing Galaxy unit tests in `test_custos_authnz.py`. 

I did add a file called `pkce_utils.py` in `galaxy/lib/galaxy/authnz`. This file is just taken from a python package (https://pypi.org/project/pkce/), and I included the license from that, because I wasn't sure how to handle the dependency management.. Let me know if this would need to be changed. I did not make changes to any of the docs. This is my first pr to this project, so let me know if I need to change anything.

The spec for PKCE is here: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7636
More info can be found here: https://www.oauth.com/oauth2-servers/pkce/


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [x] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [x] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. Make sure the OIDC support is enabled in `galaxy.yml`. Instructions are found here: https://galaxyproject.org/authnz/config/oidc/#enable-oidc-based-login 
  2. For testing with Keycloak, in `oidc_backends_config`, under the Keycloak configuration add a xml attribute called 
`pkce_support` like the following to an existing Keycloak config:
  
 ```oidc_backends_config.xml

    <provider name="Keycloak">
        <!-- The URL should include the base keycloak path as well as the realm -->
        <url>http://localhost-uri:8080/auth/realms/master/</url>
        <pkce_support>true</pkce_support>
         ...
 ```
  3. Configure Keycloak to use PKCE. Instructions can be found in the Keycloak docs by searching for PKCE: https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_oidc_clients. 
  4. Start Galaxy, and open up the home page.
  5. Click on the login link in the top right.
  6. Choose to sign in with KeyCloak. 
  7. Sign in as a Keycloak user.
  8. You should be redirected back to the Galaxy homepage and signed in.

support","user":{"login":"gecage952","id":30890419,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwODkwNDE5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30890419?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952","html_url":"https://github.com/gecage952","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Relevant Issue: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13605

This pr is to add Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) support when using OIDC auth. Essentially, it's a few extra parameters added to some OAuth requests for extra security. Some OIDC providers recommend having support for these parameters, and it may be required for some. 

I tested this pr manually with Keycloak using a setup that required PKCE parameters, and then both a Keycloak setup and a Google setup  that did not require them making sure existing functionality was not affected. I also added a unit test with the other OIDC auth tests that followed the pattern of other already existing Galaxy unit tests in `test_custos_authnz.py`. 

I did add a file called `pkce_utils.py` in `galaxy/lib/galaxy/authnz`. This file is just taken from a python package (https://pypi.org/project/pkce/), and I included the license from that, because I wasn't sure how to handle the dependency management.. Let me know if this would need to be changed. I did not make changes to any of the docs. This is my first pr to this project, so let me know if I need to change anything.

The spec for PKCE is here: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7636
More info can be found here: https://www.oauth.com/oauth2-servers/pkce/


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [x] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [x] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. Make sure the OIDC support is enabled in `galaxy.yml`. Instructions are found here: https://galaxyproject.org/authnz/config/oidc/#enable-oidc-based-login 
  2. For testing with Keycloak, in `oidc_backends_config`, under the Keycloak configuration add a xml attribute called 
`pkce_support` like the following to an existing Keycloak config:
  
 ```oidc_backends_config.xml

    <provider name="Keycloak">
        <!-- The URL should include the base keycloak path as well as the realm -->
        <url>http://localhost-uri:8080/auth/realms/master/</url>
        <pkce_support>true</pkce_support>
         ...
 ```
  3. Configure Keycloak to use PKCE. Instructions can be found in the Keycloak docs by searching for PKCE: https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_oidc_clients. 
  4. Start Galaxy, and open up the home page.
  5. Click on the login link in the top right.
  6. Choose to sign in with KeyCloak. 
  7. Sign in as a Keycloak user.
  8. You should be redirected back to the Galaxy homepage and signed in.
