l":"https://api.github.com/users/dannon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dannon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dannon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dannon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dannon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dannon/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"open_issues":129,"closed_issues":316,"state":"open","created_at":"2022-01-11T17:28:18Z","updated_at":"2022-05-09T18:28:01Z","due_on":"2022-05-01T07:00:00Z","closed_at":null},"comments":3,"created_at":"2022-04-06T19:44:05Z","updated_at":"2022-05-09T18:34:33Z","closed_at":"2022-05-09T18:28:01Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/13683","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/13683","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/13683.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/13683.patch","merged_at":"2022-05-09T18:28:01Z"},"body":"Relevant Issue: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13605

This pr is to add Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) support when using OIDC auth. Essentially, it's a few extra parameters added to some OAuth requests for extra security. Some OIDC providers recommend having support for these parameters, and it may be required for some. 

I tested this pr manually with Keycloak using a setup that required PKCE parameters, and then both a Keycloak setup and a Google setup  that did not require them making sure existing functionality was not affected. I also added a unit test with the other OIDC auth tests that followed the pattern of other already existing Galaxy unit tests in `test_custos_authnz.py`. 

I did add a file called `pkce_utils.py` in `galaxy/lib/galaxy/authnz`. This file is just taken from a python package (https://pypi.org/project/pkce/), and I included the license from that, because I wasn't sure how to handle the dependency management.. Let me know if this would need to be changed. I did not make changes to any of the docs. This is my first pr to this project, so let me know if I need to change anything.

The spec for PKCE is here: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7636
More info can be found here: https://www.oauth.com/oauth2-servers/pkce/


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [x] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [x] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. Make sure the OIDC support is enabled in `galaxy.yml`. Instructions are found here: https://galaxyproject.org/authnz/config/oidc/#enable-oidc-based-login 
  2. For testing with Keycloak, in `oidc_backends_config`, under the Keycloak configuration add a xml attribute called 
`pkce_support` like the following to an existing Keycloak config:
  
 ```oidc_backends_config.xml

    <provider name="Keycloak">
        <!-- The URL should include the base keycloak path as well as the realm -->
        <url>http://localhost-uri:8080/auth/realms/master/</url>
        <pkce_support>true</pkce_support>
         ...
 ```
  3. Configure Keycloak to use PKCE. Instructions can be found in the Keycloak docs by searching for PKCE: https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_oidc_clients. 
  4. Start Galaxy, and open up the home page.
  5. Click on the login link in the top right.
  6. Choose to sign in with KeyCloak. 
  7. Sign in as a Keycloak user.
  8. You should be redirected back to the Galaxy homepage and signed in.

{"action":"opened","number":13683,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/13683","id":901936755,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAdw-Nc41wnZz","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/13683","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/13683.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/13683.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13683","number":13683,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Add OIDC PKCE parameter support","user":{"login":"gecage952","id":30890419,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwODkwNDE5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30890419?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952","html_url":"https://github.com/gecage952","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Relevant Issue: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13605

This pr is to add Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) support when using OIDC auth. Essentially, it's a few extra parameters added to some OAuth requests for extra security. Some OIDC providers recommend having support for these parameters, and it may be required for some. 

I tested this pr manually with Keycloak using a setup that required PKCE parameters, and then both a Keycloak setup and a Google setup  that did not require them making sure existing functionality was not affected. I also added a unit test with the other OIDC auth tests that followed the pattern of other already existing Galaxy unit tests in `test_custos_authnz.py`. 

I did add a file called `pkce_utils.py` in `galaxy/lib/galaxy/authnz`. This file is just taken from a python package (https://pypi.org/project/pkce/), and I included the license from that, because I wasn't sure how to handle the dependency management.. Let me know if this would need to be changed. I did not make changes to any of the docs. This is my first pr to this project, so let me know if I need to change anything.

The spec for PKCE is here: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7636
More info can be found here: https://www.oauth.com/oauth2-servers/pkce/


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [x] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [x] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. Make sure the OIDC support is enabled in `galaxy.yml`. Instructions are found here: https://galaxyproject.org/authnz/config/oidc/#enable-oidc-based-login 
  2. For testing with Keycloak, in `oidc_backends_config`, under the Keycloak configuration add a xml attribute called 
`pkce_support` like the following to an existing Keycloak config:
  
 ```oidc_backends_config.xml

    <provider name="Keycloak">
        <!-- The URL should include the base keycloak path as well as the realm -->
        <url>http://localhost-uri:8080/auth/realms/master/</url>
        <pkce_support>true</pkce_support>
         ...
 ```
  3. Configure Keycloak to use PKCE. Instructions can be found in the Keycloak docs by searching for PKCE: https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_oidc_clients. 
  4. Start Galaxy, and open up the home page.
  5. Click on the login link in the top right.
  6. Choose to sign in with KeyCloak. 
  7. Sign in as a Keycloak user.
  8. You should be redirected back to the Galaxy homepage and signed in.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13605","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13605/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13605/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13605/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13605","id":1178632115,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5GQH-z","number":13605,"title":"Add Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) OIDC support","user":{"login":"gecage952","id":30890419,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwODkwNDE5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30890419?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952","html_url":"https://github.com/gecage952","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gecage952/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2022-03-23T20:32:18Z","updated_at":"2022-04-06T19:45:21Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hi, currently when using OIDC integration, I've found that there doesn't seem to be support for the Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) spec that some OIDC providers require or encourage. The spec is detailed here: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7636. On a local fork, I've managed to put a rough fix for it, but I wanted to open an actual issue on here about it. Essentially, in the OIDC authnz code, alongside the nonce and state parameters, a code verifier and code challenge should be generated. The code challenge would be sent with the authentication url and the verifier should be stored for verification later. When the token exchange happens, the verifier would need to be sent along with that request, which would result in getting the appropriate token. It shouldn't be too big of change, but I'm not sure how you would want to handle the configuration of whether to use PKCE or not. Let me know if you need more info. The spec is detailed here: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7636. On a local fork, I've managed to put a rough fix for it, but I wanted to open an actual issue on here about it. Essentially, in the OIDC authnz code, alongside the nonce and state parameters, a code verifier and code challenge should be generated. When the token exchange happens, the verifier would need to be sent along with that request, which would result in getting the appropriate token.
